StorybookSetupRules.keywords: storybook, setup, mcp, agentic-init, install, addon-test, agents-md
StorybookSetupRules.Mission:
Define how an execution-agent installs and configures Storybook for an agent-first project. The agent runs the official initializer, follows its generated guidance, installs the MCP/test addon set, registers the MCP server with the agent runner, and updates `AGENTS.md` so the integration is discoverable without chat context.
**Base axiom:** Storybook's value for an agentic project is mechanical — preview URL + MCP tools + run-story-tests. If any one of those three is missing, the integration is not «partially working»; it is broken in a way that silently fails later. The setup directive optimises for «all three present and verified» on first installation, not for fast partial scaffolds.
Companion (runtime usage after setup): `ai/directives/testing/storybook-usage.xml`.

Belief_State.Axiom:
Storybook installation starts with the official initializer (`npx storybook@latest init`). Manually scaffolding `.storybook/main.*`, `.storybook/preview.*`, or hand-editing build config before running `init` is forbidden — the initializer is the source of truth for the framework detection, plugin set, and TS configuration that the project's framework currently requires.
The init is interactive: when it prompts about framework, builder, or feature opt-ins, the agent answers based on observed project state (existing `vite.config.*`, `package.json` scripts, framework deps), not on assumptions.

After `init` completes, the generated setup notes (`storybook` CLI output + any `.storybook/` README or onboarding file) are normative. The agent does NOT improvise alternative configuration, alternative entry points, or alternative scripts; it follows the generated guide and only deviates with explicit operator confirmation. Drift between the installer's intent and the project's actual config is the most common source of «Storybook used to work» tickets.

For an agent-first project, the MCP integration (test/preview addon exposing tools the agent runner can call) is NOT optional. Without MCP, the agent cannot fetch component docs, preview stories, or run story tests programmatically — Storybook becomes a human-only GUI tool and loses its value in the workflow.
Setup is incomplete until the MCP addon is installed AND the MCP server is registered with the agent runner AND at least one MCP tool call returns a non-error response.

Setup ends with an `AGENTS.md` update making the integration discoverable: which MCP tools exist, story-file conventions adopted, tags reserved (e.g. `ai-generated`), pointer to the runtime usage directive. Skipping the `AGENTS.md` step turns the integration into tribal knowledge — every later agent must rediscover it from scratch.

Setup is verified end-to-end before being declared complete: 1. `storybook dev` boots without errors and serves the preview URL. 2. At least one MCP tool call to the registered server succeeds. 3. `run-story-tests` (or the project's equivalent) passes on at least one story.
Any of the three failing → setup is not done; the failure is surfaced to the operator with the exact error rather than masked as «mostly working».

Story tests run in a real browser (typically Chromium via Playwright). If the project does not already install Playwright browsers, the setup MUST add the install step (`npx playwright install chromium`, or the framework's equivalent) and verify the browser is available before declaring done. A missing browser turns the first story-test run into a confusing failure.

Story-level tests use ONE harness — the test addon shipped/recommended with Storybook for the project's framework. Introducing a parallel harness (e.g., a custom Vitest project that mounts stories outside the addon) fragments the project's testing surface and creates two report channels for the same component contract. If the project already has a chosen Storybook test addon, the agent reuses it; if not, the agent installs the official one and stops there.

Definitions.Definition: An MCP-compliant server exposing Storybook capabilities (documentation retrieval, story preview, story-test execution) to an agent runner. Realised by the Storybook test/MCP addon plus runner-side registration.

The Storybook addon that provides programmatic story execution and assertions (current canonical: `@storybook/experimental-addon-test` family or its successor). Used by both human-side test runs and the MCP server.

Setup_Steps.Step: Run `npx storybook@latest init` and answer prompts based on observed project structure. Do NOT pre-scaffold `.storybook/` before this step. Ref: AX_SB_INIT_VIA_OFFICIAL_INSTALLER.
Apply every manual step in the initializer's generated guide / onboarding doc verbatim. Surface deviations to the operator before applying them. Ref: AX_SB_FOLLOW_GENERATED_GUIDE.

Install the Storybook test/MCP addon recommended for the framework: `npx storybook add @storybook/experimental-addon-test` (or its current canonical successor). Confirm the addon appears in `package.json` and in `.storybook/main.*` addons array. Ref: AX_SB_MCP_IS_MANDATORY, AX_SB_NO_PARALLEL_TEST_HARNESSES.
If a story-test browser is not already installed, run `npx playwright install chromium` (or the project's equivalent install step) and verify availability. Ref: AX_SB_BROWSER_PREREQ_HANDLED.
Register the Storybook MCP server in the agent runner's MCP configuration (command, args, env). Restart / reload the runner if it requires it. Ref: AX_SB_MCP_IS_MANDATORY.
Boot `storybook dev`, confirm preview URL responds; make at least one MCP tool call; run `run-story-tests` on one story. All three must succeed. Ref: AX_SB_VERIFY_BEFORE_HANDOFF.

Add a Storybook section to `AGENTS.md`: MCP tools available, story-file conventions (`*.stories.ts` location, naming), reserved tags (`ai-generated`), pointer to `testing/storybook-usage.xml`. Ref: AX_SB_AGENTS_MD_UPDATED_AS_PART_OF_SETUP.

Anti_Pattern.Bad: Agent hand-creates `.storybook/main.ts` with a guess at the framework integration, copies a preset from another project, and runs `storybook dev` to «see if it works».
Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: Bypasses the official initializer (`AX_SB_INIT_VIA_OFFICIAL_INSTALLER`). The initializer's framework detection is the single source of truth for the current Storybook version's expected config; a hand-rolled main file drifts from it on the first minor upgrade and produces opaque «works on the laptop, fails in CI» symptoms.
Anti_Pattern.Good: Delete the hand-rolled `.storybook/`, run `npx storybook@latest init`, answer prompts based on observed project state, then follow the generated guide verbatim.

Anti_Pattern.Bad: Agent installs base Storybook, gets `storybook dev` running, and marks the ticket done without installing the test/MCP addon or registering the MCP server.
Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: Storybook present but unreachable by agents (`AX_SB_MCP_IS_MANDATORY`). Every subsequent component task must either rediscover props from source code or fall back to human-driven preview. The integration is structurally incomplete; calling it «done» misrepresents project state.
Anti_Pattern.Good: Install the recommended test/MCP addon (`STEP_SB_3_INSTALL_TEST_ADDON`), register the MCP server with the agent runner (`STEP_SB_5_REGISTER_MCP_SERVER`), and verify at least one MCP tool call returns a non-error response.

Anti_Pattern.Bad: Agent finishes the install steps and reports «Storybook configured» based solely on `npm install` exit code, without booting `storybook dev` or running any story test.
Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: Setup declared complete without end-to-end verification (`AX_SB_VERIFY_BEFORE_HANDOFF`). Misses Playwright browser missing, MCP server not registered, addon misconfigured, port conflict — all of which compile fine but break the first real use.
Anti_Pattern.Good: Boot `storybook dev` and confirm preview URL responds; call one MCP tool; run `run-story-tests` on one story. If any fails, surface the exact error to the operator before marking the ticket complete.

Anti_Pattern.Bad: Setup verified end-to-end, but `AGENTS.md` not updated — no mention of Storybook tooling, MCP tools, or story conventions.
Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: Integration becomes tribal knowledge (`AX_SB_AGENTS_MD_UPDATED_AS_PART_OF_SETUP`). Next agent on the project must rediscover the entire integration from chat history or trial-and-error. Defeats the «discoverable without chat context» principle that justifies `AGENTS.md` in the first place.
Anti_Pattern.Good: Append a Storybook section to `AGENTS.md`: MCP tool names, story-file location and naming conventions, reserved tags (e.g. `ai-generated`), pointer to `testing/storybook-usage.xml`.

Hook.Purpose: Storybook dev server boots without errors and serves the preview.
Hook.Command: npx storybook dev --ci --quiet & PID=$!; sleep 8; curl -fsS http://localhost:6006/index.json > /dev/null; STATUS=$?; kill $PID 2>/dev/null; wait $PID 2>/dev/null; exit $STATUS
Hook.Expected: Exit 0. Non-zero indicates the preview did not boot within the timeout or the index endpoint is unreachable.

Hook.Purpose: Test/MCP addon is listed in Storybook config.
Hook.Expected: At least one match. Empty output means the addon is not registered in `.storybook/main.*`.

Hook.Purpose: `AGENTS.md` mentions Storybook so the integration is discoverable.
Hook.Command: test -f AGENTS.md && grep -nE 'Storybook|storybook' AGENTS.md || echo MISSING
Hook.Expected: At least one match in `AGENTS.md`. `MISSING` (file absent) or empty output is a setup gap.

Hook.Purpose: Chromium is installed for story tests.
Hook.Command: npx playwright install --dry-run chromium 2>&1 | grep -E "browser is already installed|is downloaded" || echo MISSING
Hook.Expected: Output indicates Chromium is already installed. `MISSING` means the browser still needs `npx playwright install chromium`.

StorybookSetupRules.Reward_Criteria:
✅ Storybook installed via the official initializer; generated guide applied verbatim. ✅ Test/MCP addon installed and present in `.storybook/main.*`. ✅ MCP server registered in the agent runner; at least one MCP tool call succeeds. ✅ Required browser (Chromium / equivalent) installed before story-test runs. ✅ End-to-end verification: `storybook dev` boots, MCP tool call returns, `run-story-tests` passes on one story. ✅ `AGENTS.md` updated with Storybook section: MCP tools, story conventions, reserved tags, pointer to `testing/storybook-usage.xml`.
❌ Hand-scaffolded `.storybook/` config bypassing the initializer. ❌ Storybook installed without the test/MCP addon. ❌ MCP server not registered with the agent runner. ❌ Setup declared done without booting `storybook dev` or running a story test. ❌ `AGENTS.md` not updated; integration left as tribal knowledge. ❌ Parallel story-test harness introduced alongside the official addon.
